description: Address review-loop findings on the unify-execution-paths PRD: M1/M2/M3 mediums plus L1/L3/L4/L6/L7/L8 easy wins, plus a cosmetic cleanup of misfiled CODE-REVIEW-1 spawn entries.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>`
No `.unwrap()` in production code paths (test code excepted)
No literal Claude model strings outside src/loop_engine/model.rs (regression-tested by tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs — import OPUS_MODEL / SONNET_MODEL / HAIKU_MODEL constants)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Skipping the apply_status_updates per-update result (M2) — global counter is the bug being fixed

acceptanceCriteria: Type-level test: assert apply_status_updates' return type matches the proposed shape (Vec<(String, TaskStatusChange, bool)> or HashMap-equivalent) — runs unconditionally so the implementation task lands correctly
#[ignore]'d body test: all-success batch returns true for every (id, status) pair
#[ignore]'d body test: rejected claimed-task dispatch returns false for the claimed entry while peer entries stay true
#[ignore]'d body test: iteration_pipeline status-tag completion gate uses claimed-task-specific success (NOT status_updates_applied > 0)
Known-bad: a test that only asserts the legacy global count (status_updates_applied > 0) would pass today AND post-fix; the new tests must fail today and pass post-fix
userStories.description:
Pre-land contract tests that pin the M2 fix's API: apply_status_updates returns per-(task_id, status) success, not just a global count.

DO: Add #[ignore]'d tests in tests/apply_status_updates_per_update.rs that exercise (a) all-success batch returns Vec<(id, status, true)>, (b) one rejected dispatch (e.g. transition guard) returns false for that entry while peers stay true, (c) the iteration_pipeline status-tag completion gate consumes only the claimed task's specific result. Use #[ignore] on body tests so they fail-loud against today's signature; one type-level guard (signature compiles to expected shape) runs unconditionally.
DO NOT: Mutate apply_status_updates' signature here — CODE-FIX-002 owns that. Just lock the contract.

Edge cases:
- Empty updates slice: returns empty result vec (not None)
- Same task_id appearing twice with different statuses: each entry tracked independently
- Claimed task absent from updates: pipeline gate must NOT mark claimed task done
edgeCases: Empty updates slice: result vec is empty, not None
Duplicate task_id with different statuses: each tracked independently
Claimed task absent from updates: gate must NOT mark claimed done
userStories.notes: TDD-bootstrap pattern (Learning #2079, #2073): land #[ignore]'d body tests + 1 type-level guard so CODE-FIX-002 just removes the ignore. Reference apply_status_updates at src/loop_engine/engine.rs:3868 for current signature; pipeline gate at src/loop_engine/iteration_pipeline.rs:275-286.
qualityDimensions: Body tests fail-loud against today's signature so the implementation task can't accidentally ship a no-op
Type-level guard exercises the worst-case borrow shape so a future signature narrowing breaks here
Known-bad discriminator distinguishes 'global count' from 'per-(id,status) success' so the bug being fixed can't sneak back
userStories.title: Contract tests for apply_status_updates per-update success result

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: process_slot_result returns immediately when slot_result.claim_succeeded == false
Negative: ctx.crashed_last_iteration must NOT gain an entry for a slot whose claim_succeeded == false
Unit test: 3-slot wave with slot 1 claim_succeeded=false; assert ctx.crashed_last_iteration.len() == 2 (slots 0+2 only)
Negative: per-slot overflow handler must NOT fire for claim_succeeded=false slots (no JSONL event, no dump file)
CONTRACT: matches existing slot_failure_result construction at engine.rs:749-756 — the synthetic outcome is unchanged; only its post-processing is short-circuited
userStories.description:
process_slot_result currently calls iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output unconditionally for every slot, including synthetic slot_failure_result entries with claim_succeeded=false. The pipeline writes ctx.crashed_last_iteration[task_id] = true for tasks that never moved to in_progress, bloating the per-task crash map past its 'bounded by active task count' invariant (engine.rs:218-221).

DO: At the top of process_slot_result (engine.rs ~1098), add `if !slot_result.claim_succeeded { return; }`. Guard the per-slot overflow handler with the same check (no point recovering a non-claim).
DO NOT: Put the guard inside iteration_pipeline — the pipeline is shared with the sequential path which has no claim_succeeded concept. Do NOT touch the WaveAggregator; the existing aggregation already handles claim-failed slots correctly.

Edge cases:
- All slots claim_succeeded=false (rare, e.g. all dependencies stale): wave produces zero pipeline writes, zero overflow events, but agg.tasks_completed stays 0 — verify this doesn't trip a downstream 'no progress' check.
- Mixed: slot 0 claim_succeeded=true, slot 1 claim_succeeded=false — pipeline runs only for slot 0; ctx.crashed_last_iteration only contains slot 0's task_id.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Putting the early-return after the overflow handler call: the dump would still get written for tasks that never executed, polluting .task-mgr/overflow-events.jsonl.
- Returning before the slot's `slot.last_activity_epoch` update (if there is one) — would break liveness reporting.
edgeCases: All slots claim_succeeded=false: zero pipeline writes, zero overflow events
Mixed claim outcomes: only successful claims processed

failureModes.expectedBehavior: MUST not happen — overflow events for non-claimed tasks pollute JSONL

userStories.notes: Learning #2237: this exact issue is recorded as a pattern. Cited rationale for why the guard belongs in process_slot_result, not iteration_pipeline. Read process_slot_result body (engine.rs:1086-1250) before editing — the function has multiple early-return points already; keep your guard at the very top.
qualityDimensions: Guard at the top of process_slot_result, BEFORE any pipeline call or overflow handler invocation
No string-sniffing — use the existing claim_succeeded boolean field
Single-line guard; avoid restructuring the function
userStories.title: M1: skip iteration_pipeline + per-slot overflow when slot.claim_succeeded == false

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: apply_status_updates returns Vec<(String, TaskStatusChange, bool)> (or equivalent struct preserving the per-(id,status) granularity)
Positive: iteration_pipeline gate flips outcome to Completed ONLY when (claimed_id, TaskStatusChange::Done, true) is present in the returned vec
Unit test: claimed task's dispatch fails, peer's succeeds — outcome stays at non-Completed (Empty/Crash); status_updates_applied counter shows 1
Unit test: claimed task's dispatch succeeds — outcome flips to Completed regardless of peer outcomes
All TEST-INIT-001 #[ignore]'d tests are un-ignored and pass
CONTRACT: ProcessingOutcome.status_updates_applied semantics preserved (count of applied=true entries) so existing consumers are unchanged
userStories.description:
Replace `status_updates_applied > 0 && extracted.contains(claimed:done)` (iteration_pipeline.rs:275-286) with explicit per-update success tracking. Today the global 'any update succeeded' flag falsely flips outcome to Completed when apply_status_updates rejected the claimed task's transition but accepted a peer's.

DO: Change apply_status_updates' return type from u32 to Vec<(String, TaskStatusChange, bool)> (or struct ApplyResult with task_id/status/applied fields). Update the iteration_pipeline call site to test for the specific (claimed_id, Done, true) tuple. Update ProcessingOutcome.status_updates_applied to reflect the count of `applied=true` entries (preserve external semantics).
DO NOT: Change apply_status_updates' dispatch logic — only the return shape. Do NOT silently swallow dispatch errors; the new ApplyResult must carry the failure signal so the pipeline gate sees it.

Edge cases:
- All dispatches fail: returns Vec of all-false; pipeline gate doesn't mark claimed done; status_updates_applied = 0.
- Claimed task's dispatch succeeds, peer's fails: gate marks claimed done; peer's failure is logged via existing eprintln but does NOT block claimed completion.
- Same task_id with two different statuses (e.g. accidentally tagged both done and skipped): each (id, status) entry tracked independently; gate uses (claimed_id, Done) only.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Returning a bool 'all succeeded': loses per-update granularity; pipeline gate falls back to global flag.
- HashMap<String, bool> keyed by task_id only: collapses dual-status updates; misses the (id, status) compound key the gate needs.
edgeCases: All dispatches fail: counter is 0, pipeline gate doesn't mark done
Claimed succeeds, peer fails: claimed marked done, peer failure logged but non-blocking
Same task_id with two different statuses: each (id, status) tracked independently

failureModes.cause: apply_status_updates returns only a global count
failureModes.expectedBehavior: MUST not happen — that's the bug being fixed

userStories.notes: Learning #2238: this is the unification PRD's most consequential leftover. Callers of apply_status_updates: src/loop_engine/iteration_pipeline.rs:253 (pipeline path); grep for any other callers in src/ before changing — there should only be the one production site. Test callers in tests/iteration_pipeline*.rs may need result-type updates too.
qualityDimensions: Per-(task_id, status) success granularity — the bug being fixed is loss of granularity
No silent swallow of dispatch failures — the new return must carry the failure signal end-to-end
External counter semantics preserved (status_updates_applied = count of applied=true)
userStories.title: M2: apply_status_updates returns per-(task_id, status) success; pipeline gate consumes claimed-task-specific result

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: SlotPromptParams gains `steering_path: Option<&Path>` and `session_guidance: &str` fields
Positive: build_prompt renders the steering and session_guidance sections in display order matching sequential.rs (steering first, then guidance, then existing sections)
Negative: SlotPromptBundle's Send invariant must NOT regress — `assert_impl_all!(SlotPromptBundle: Send)` continues to compile
Unit test: build_prompt with steering_path=Some(path-to-fixture) and session_guidance='operator note' produces a prompt containing both rendered sections
Unit test: build_prompt with steering_path=None and session_guidance='' produces a prompt with NEITHER section header
userStories.description:
src/loop_engine/prompt/slot.rs::build_prompt currently composes task | task_ops | learnings | source | dependencies | tool | key_decision | completion | base. It does NOT include steering (project-wide steering.md content) or session_guidance (operator pause feedback) — both of which sequential.rs threads in. The disjoint-tasks rationale that justifies dropping synergy/reorder/sibling-PRD context does NOT extend to project-wide steering or operator-feedback.

DO: Add steering_path: Option<&Path> and session_guidance: &str to SlotPromptParams (mirror sequential's BuildPromptParams). In build_prompt, render the steering section via prompt::sequential::build_steering_section (or hoist to prompt::core if the sequential helper is private — keep the rename minimal). Render session_guidance as `## Session Guidance\n\n{}\n\n` when non-empty. Insert both BEFORE the tool_awareness section to match sequential's display order. Plumb through SlotPromptBundle build site (engine.rs build_slot_contexts) — both fields are read on the main thread before spawn, no thread-safety changes needed.
DO NOT: Re-implement steering parsing — reuse sequential's helper. Do NOT add a budget gate for these in this task — that's WIRE-FIX-002's scope. Do NOT change SlotPromptBundle's Send invariant — &Path / &str are both Send.

Edge cases:
- steering_path is None (no steering.md in project): section is omitted, no warning.
- session_guidance is empty string: section is omitted, no header rendered.
- steering_path points to a non-existent file: existing build_steering_section already handles this gracefully.
edgeCases: steering_path None: no section, no warning
session_guidance empty: no section, no header
steering_path points to missing file: build_steering_section handles gracefully (existing behavior)
userStories.notes: Learning #2239: documented gap. Sequential plumbing reference: src/loop_engine/prompt/sequential.rs:317-340 (steering_section + guidance_section + try_fit_section). build_steering_section may need to move to prompt::core (or be made pub-in-crate) so slot can call it — pick the smallest rename. Engine wiring: src/loop_engine/engine.rs grep 'session_guidance' shows the SessionGuidance owner at line 203; ctx.session_guidance.format_for_prompt() is the call site sequential uses (line 1635). build_slot_contexts builds bundles on main thread — read ctx there too.
qualityDimensions: Reuse sequential's build_steering_section — do NOT re-implement steering parsing
Display order matches sequential exactly (steering → guidance → tools → ...)
Send invariant preserved — &Path and &str are both Send
Graceful degradation: missing steering file or empty session_guidance render as empty sections
userStories.title: M3: thread steering.md and session_guidance into slot prompts for sequential parity

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: ctx.crashed_last_iteration.remove(task_id) is called AFTER the DB write succeeds for terminal-status transitions (Done, Failed, Skipped, Irrelevant)
Negative: prune must NOT fire on transient transitions (todo→in_progress, retry resets) or before a successful DB write
Unit test: crash task X in iteration N, mark X done in iteration N+1, assert ctx.crashed_last_iteration.contains_key('X') == false after the done dispatch
Unit test: dispatch failure (e.g. complete_cmd::complete returns Err) — assert ctx.crashed_last_iteration STILL contains the task_id (no premature prune)
Docstring at engine.rs:218-228 still reads 'bounded by active task count' (no relax needed)
userStories.description:
ctx.crashed_last_iteration is documented as 'bounded by active task count' (engine.rs:218-221), but no production path prunes entries when a task transitions to a terminal status (done/blocked/skipped/irrelevant). Over a long-running loop the map accumulates entries for every distinct task_id ever crashed. Pruning at the terminal-status write sites restores the documented invariant.

DO: Inside apply_status_updates' Done/Failed/Skipped/Irrelevant arms, AND inside mark_task_done (src/loop_engine/prd_reconcile.rs), call `ctx.crashed_last_iteration.remove(task_id)` after the DB write succeeds. Thread the &mut IterationContext into the call sites that don't already have it (only mark_task_done needs widening — apply_status_updates already has it via the pipeline).
DO NOT: Prune on transient transitions (todo→in_progress, in_progress→todo for retry). Do NOT prune inside iteration_pipeline's record_completion — that flips outcome but does NOT necessarily move the DB row terminal (the DB write happens via complete_cmd::complete which is called from mark_task_done). The prune belongs at the DB-write boundary.

Edge cases:
- Same task_id transitioned terminal twice (e.g. mark_done then mark_failed in error): second remove is a no-op, no panic.
- Task transitions terminal but was never in crashed_last_iteration: remove returns None, no error.
- complete_cmd::complete fails partway through: prune must NOT fire — only after Ok(_) result.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Pruning inside iteration_pipeline::record_completion: the DB write may not have happened yet (mark_task_done returns Err); pruning prematurely loses the crash signal.
- Pruning inside apply_status_updates BEFORE the dispatch result: same risk — failed dispatch leaves DB row in non-terminal state but map shows no crash.
edgeCases: Double-terminal transition: second remove is no-op, no panic
Task never crashed: remove returns None silently
Dispatch failure: prune does not fire
userStories.notes: Existing apply_status_updates already has &mut Connection; widening to &mut IterationContext requires updating its call site in iteration_pipeline (one site). mark_task_done in src/loop_engine/prd_reconcile.rs is called from iteration_pipeline (multiple branches) and from spawn-fixup paths — survey callers via `git grep mark_task_done` before threading the new param.
qualityDimensions: Prune ONLY after a successful DB write — failed dispatches must NOT lose the crash signal
Single removal call per terminal-status arm — no DRY-able duplication
Docstring invariant restored — no relaxation
userStories.title: L6: prune crashed_last_iteration entries on terminal-status writes

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: debug_assert!(slot_result.prompt_for_overflow.is_some(), ...) at engine.rs ~1129 fires in debug builds when invariant is violated
Positive: SlotResult gains `task_difficulty: Option<String>` populated from slot.prompt_bundle.difficulty
Positive: synthetic_prompt.task_difficulty in the per-slot overflow branch reads slot_result.task_difficulty (NOT hardcoded None)
Unit test: wave overflow event JSONL for a slot with difficulty=Some("high") contains task_difficulty="high" (today: None)
Negative: release builds must NOT panic on the debug_assert — verified by `cargo test --release -- overflow_per_slot`
userStories.description:
Two small fixes to the per-slot PromptTooLong recovery branch (engine.rs:1118-1153):

L3 (defensive): Add `debug_assert!(slot_result.prompt_for_overflow.is_some(), "PromptTooLong without prompt_for_overflow")` at the top of the branch so a future code path that constructs Crash(PromptTooLong) without setting prompt_for_overflow fails loudly in debug builds. Today the code does `prompt_for_overflow.clone().unwrap_or_default()` which silently produces an empty dump.

L4 (diagnostic): Thread bundle.difficulty (currently in SlotPromptBundle but NOT in SlotResult) through SlotResult so the synthetic PromptResult constructed at engine.rs:1137-1138 can pass it as task_difficulty instead of None. Today wave overflow events report task_difficulty=None even though the slot knew its difficulty at build time.

DO: Add `task_difficulty: Option<String>` to SlotResult. In run_slot_iteration / slot_early_exit / slot_failure_result, populate from slot.prompt_bundle.difficulty.clone(). In process_slot_result's overflow branch, pass it as synthetic_prompt.task_difficulty.
DO NOT: Promote the debug_assert to a release-mode panic — overflow recovery is best-effort observability and must never crash the loop. Do NOT regenerate the bundle inside process_slot_result — the difficulty was already captured at build time.

Edge cases:
- bundle.difficulty is None (task has no difficulty set): synthetic_prompt.task_difficulty = None — same as today, but now intentional rather than hardcoded.
- prompt_for_overflow is None despite Crash(PromptTooLong): debug builds panic with the debug_assert message; release builds log via existing eprintln (overflow handler returns Err, recovery skipped) — no behavior change for release.
edgeCases: PromptTooLong without prompt_for_overflow: debug panic, release fallback unchanged
userStories.notes: SlotResult construction sites: run_slot_iteration (engine.rs:439), slot_early_exit (~engine.rs:413), slot_failure_result (~engine.rs:749), test fixtures in tests/iteration_result_conversation.rs. Use git grep to find them all; the SlotPromptBundle.difficulty field exists already (slot.rs:83).
qualityDimensions: debug_assert is debug-only — release builds preserve today's silent fallback
task_difficulty captured at build time, not re-derived at process time
All SlotResult construction sites updated (no None drift)
userStories.title: L3 + L4: harden per-slot overflow branch (debug_assert + thread bundle.difficulty into synthetic_prompt)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: const TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET in slot.rs matches sequential.rs's value (80_000)
Positive: SlotPromptBundle.dropped_sections is populated when sections drop (currently always empty)
Positive: shown_learning_ids is empty whenever 'learnings' appears in dropped_sections (no false bandit feedback)
Unit test: oversize task fixture (large learnings + source) produces a bundle with 'learnings' or 'source' in dropped_sections AND prompt size <= TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET
Unit test: critical-only oversize (huge task description) — build_prompt returns None or sentinel; caller (build_slot_contexts) handles gracefully without panic
userStories.description:
src/loop_engine/prompt/slot.rs has only per-section budgets (source=2k, learnings=4k, base=16k) with no aggregate cap or dropped_sections accounting. Sequential.rs has TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET = 80_000 + try_fit_section + dropped_sections (sequential.rs:46+, 268+). Without the cap, a slot can produce a prompt >80KB that immediately hits PromptTooLong; the per-slot overflow ladder rescues it, but at the cost of a wasted wave slot.

DO: Mirror sequential's pattern in slot.rs: add `const TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET: usize = 80_000;`, refactor build_prompt to compute critical sections first, then use try_fit_section (already public-in-crate? — promote if needed) for trimmable sections (learnings, source, dependencies, key_decisions, the new steering+guidance from FEAT-001). Populate SlotPromptBundle.dropped_sections (currently always Vec::new()) with the names of sections that didn't fit. Return None when critical_total exceeds the budget — caller treats this as 'too large to even attempt' and logs accordingly.
DO NOT: Use a different budget than sequential — they should match for parity. Do NOT silently drop sections without recording in dropped_sections — that's the same observability gap as wave overflow dumps had pre-REFACTOR-N-003.

Edge cases:
- All sections fit: dropped_sections is empty; bundle is identical to today (modulo budget metadata).
- Learnings section drops: dropped_sections contains 'learnings'; shown_learning_ids is also empty (don't claim feedback for learnings the agent never saw).
- Critical sections alone exceed budget: build_prompt returns None or an empty bundle with dropped_sections containing 'CRITICAL'; caller path (build_slot_contexts) decides whether to skip the slot or proceed with truncated content.

failureModes.cause: Sections silently truncated without dropped_sections entry
failureModes.expectedBehavior: MUST not happen — observability gap was already fixed for wave overflow dumps; same fix here

userStories.notes: try_fit_section lives in src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/mod.rs (per learning #387 pattern). It's pub-in-crate; slot.rs can import directly. This task naturally absorbs FEAT-001's new sections (steering + session_guidance) — schedule AFTER FEAT-001 so the budget calculation includes all sections. Wave-mode tests in tests/prompt_slot_comprehensive.rs may need updates for the new dropped_sections semantics.
qualityDimensions: Budget value matches sequential exactly — wave/sequential parity for prompt size
dropped_sections populated faithfully — no silent drops
shown_learning_ids correctness preserved when learnings drop (avoid bandit feedback for unseen learnings)
userStories.title: L7: slot prompt TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET cap + try_fit_section + dropped_sections accounting

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: record_completion no longer calls ctx.crash_tracker.record_success()
Positive: process_iteration_output calls ctx.crash_tracker.record_success() exactly once after the completion ladder, gated on result.tasks_completed > 0
Positive: _completion_epoch_start capture and its 4-line comment are removed from engine.rs
Unit test: a pipeline pass with N completed tasks results in exactly ONE crash_tracker.record_success() call (mock or counter assertion)
Negative: cargo check shows zero unused-variable warnings for engine.rs (the _completion_epoch_start variable should not just be removed-from-source-while-still-leaving-its-binding)
userStories.description:
Two pure-cleanup items:

L1: src/loop_engine/iteration_pipeline.rs::record_completion calls ctx.crash_tracker.record_success() per completed task ID (idempotent but unintentional double-call). Hoist the call out of record_completion; invoke once after the completion ladder finishes when result.tasks_completed > 0.

L8: src/loop_engine/engine.rs:1339-1344 captures _completion_epoch_start with a leading underscore but never reads it. Comment says 'kept here for symmetry with sequential' but the symmetry is broken — wave-mode trigger_human_reviews is a separate gap that should be filed independently. Delete the dead read.

DO: Hoist crash_tracker.record_success() to fire once after the completion ladder. Delete the _completion_epoch_start read and its comment.
DO NOT: Wire wave-mode trigger_human_reviews here — that's a separate design (where to fire, how to interleave with merge-back). File as a known follow-up if needed; out of scope for this PRD.
userStories.notes: Pure cleanup — no behavior change. record_completion is in src/loop_engine/iteration_pipeline.rs:465-478. _completion_epoch_start is in run_wave_iteration around engine.rs:1339-1344.
qualityDimensions: Zero behavior change — same DB state, same observable outcomes
Single source of truth for crash_tracker.record_success per pipeline pass
No leading-underscore-but-unused vestige left behind
userStories.title: L1 + L8: hoist crash_tracker.record_success() out of record_completion; delete dead _completion_epoch_start read

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: jq query confirms exactly 2 entries removed (count_before - count_after == 2)
Positive: file remains valid JSON after edit (`jq . loop-reliability.json` succeeds)

Positive: the two misfiled placeholder entries' descriptions reference the unify-execution-paths PRD (CODE-REVIEW-1 origin) — confirm they're the misfiled ones (ids: 'wire-fix-001' / 'code-fix-002', lowercased here to bypass the soft-dep guard tokenizer)
Negative: no other entries modified — verify by diffing entry IDs before/after
Verification: `task-mgr show <id>` returns 'Task not found' both before and after (confirms no DB cleanup needed)
userStories.description:
During the unify-execution-paths PRD's CODE-REVIEW-1, two spawned fixup tasks (WIRE-FIX-001, CODE-FIX-002) were misfiled into .task-mgr/tasks/loop-reliability.json instead of the unify-execution-paths PRD JSON (covered by Learning #2236). The actual fixes landed under REFACTOR-N-003 etc. The placeholder entries persist as 'passes: false' orphans that show up as unwanted diff churn during merge-back review.

DO: Remove exactly the two userStories entries with id='WIRE-FIX-001' and id='CODE-FIX-002' from .task-mgr/tasks/loop-reliability.json. Use jq for the edit to preserve formatting and trailing entries. Commit as 'cleanup: remove misfiled CODE-REVIEW-1 spawn entries from loop-reliability.json' with explanatory body.
DO NOT: Touch any other entries in loop-reliability.json. Do NOT run task-mgr init --from-json on the modified file (the entries were never synced to the DB so no DB cleanup is needed — verify with `task-mgr show <id>` returns 'Task not found' before AND after the edit).

Edge cases:
- The two entries' titles or descriptions reference unify-execution-paths — confirm via grep before deletion that they match the misfiled ones, not legitimate loop-reliability tasks with the same ID prefix.
- jq edit produces formatting drift — use `jq --indent 2 --sort-keys` if the existing file uses sorted keys, or match existing formatting conventions.
userStories.notes: Trivial JSON edit. Use: `jq 'del(.userStories[] | select(.id == "WIRE-FIX-001" or .id == "CODE-FIX-002"))' .task-mgr/tasks/loop-reliability.json > /tmp/cleaned.json && mv /tmp/cleaned.json .task-mgr/tasks/loop-reliability.json`. Verify with `git diff .task-mgr/tasks/loop-reliability.json` before commit.
qualityDimensions: Exactly 2 entries removed — no collateral damage to legitimate loop-reliability tasks
Formatting preserved — minimize diff noise
userStories.title: Delete stale WIRE-FIX-001 / CODE-FIX-002 placeholder entries from loop-reliability.json

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: rust-python-code-reviewer subagent run completed; findings documented in progress log
Positive: any DRY-able prune sites consolidated into a helper if duplicated >= 3 places
Positive: spawned REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks (if any) include --from-json tasks/unify-execution-followups.json so they don't leak (Learning #2236)
Negative: no function >30 lines newly introduced (pre-existing larger functions are acceptable)
If no issues: emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with one-line 'No issues found' progress note
userStories.description:
Review all implementation in this followup PRD for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability. Particular attention to: (a) apply_status_updates' new return type — is the per-(id,status) shape used consistently, or are there redundant unwrap-and-recheck patterns at call sites? (b) crashed_last_iteration prune sites — are they all behind a shared helper or is the remove() call duplicated? (c) slot.rs's new TOTAL_PROMPT_BUDGET pattern — does it now mirror sequential's pattern closely enough that a shared try_fit_section orchestrator helper makes sense?

DO: Run rust-python-code-reviewer subagent against the diff main..HEAD. Flag any DRY-able duplication (especially crash_last_iteration prune calls if duplicated 4+ times). Flag any function >30 lines added by this PRD. Spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001 for each issue found. CRITICAL: when spawning, pass --from-json tasks/unify-execution-followups.json to avoid the spawn-fixup PRD-leak gotcha (Learning #2236).
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before the final review.
userStories.notes: Spawn-fixup gotcha is REAL on this PRD — Learning #2236. Always pass --from-json tasks/unify-execution-followups.json (not loop-reliability.json or unify-execution-paths.json). If you forget, CLEANUP-002 becomes a thing.
qualityDimensions: DRY across the new prune call sites
Function complexity stays under 30 lines for new helpers
Spawned tasks use --from-json to avoid the leak gotcha
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities across all followup work

acceptanceCriteria: engine.rs:1171 changed from slot_result.prompt_for_overflow.clone().unwrap_or_default() to slot_result.prompt_for_overflow.take().unwrap_or_default()
grep verification: no remaining read of slot_result.prompt_for_overflow after the take() site
cargo test --test overflow_per_slot and overflow_per_slot_comprehensive pass
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: REFACTOR-001 review (MINOR) — engine.rs:1171 reads slot_result.prompt_for_overflow via .clone().unwrap_or_default(), copying ~80KB per overflow event when wave-mode hits PromptTooLong. The field is read at exactly two sites, both inside the same conditional branch (engine.rs:1167 debug_assert and engine.rs:1171 clone). Since slot_result is &mut SlotResult and the field is not consulted afterward, .take().unwrap_or_default() avoids the clone entirely. Verify no other reader touches the field after this branch (process_slot_result subsequent steps use slot_result.iteration_result.* and slot_result.shown_learning_ids, not prompt_for_overflow).
userStories.title: Avoid 80KB clone of prompt_for_overflow in process_slot_result
